name: United Kingdom
metaTitle: E-2 Visa for British Nationals | Watson Immigration Law
metaDescription: British nationals have one of the oldest E-2 treaty relationships with the U.S., dating to 1815. Guidance on E-2 Treaty Investor Visa for UK nationals — visa validity, London consulate, post-Brexit passport considerations, investment requirements.
intro: The United Kingdom has one of the longest-standing treaty relationships with the United States, with treaty commerce provisions dating back to 1815. British nationals enjoy 5-year E-2 visa stamps and a straightforward consular process at the U.S. Embassy in London. Watson Immigration Law regularly represents British entrepreneurs, investors, and business owners seeking to establish or expand operations in the United States.

sections.heading: Treaty Status & History
sections.body: The U.S.-UK treaty relationship for E-2 purposes is rooted in the Treaty of Commerce and Navigation of 1815, one of the oldest bilateral treaties in the E-2 program. This long-standing relationship means British nationals have consistent, well-established E-2 access with no uncertainty about treaty eligibility.

sections.heading: Post-Brexit Passport Considerations
sections.body: Following Brexit, British nationals retain full E-2 treaty access based on their UK nationality. The E-2 visa is based on nationality — not EU residency or EU passport status — so Brexit has no effect on UK nationals' E-2 eligibility. British nationals born in England, Scotland, Wales, Northern Ireland, or any British Overseas Territory (Gibraltar, British Virgin Islands, etc.) who hold UK passports are eligible. Dual UK-Irish nationals may apply on either passport.

sections.heading: Visa Validity & Processing
sections.body: British nationals receive a 5-year E-2 visa stamp, with 2-year admissions per entry. The U.S. Embassy in London processes E-2 applications with typical interview scheduling of 4–8 weeks. Processing is generally predictable and efficient for well-documented applications.

sections.heading: Common UK Investor Business Types
sections.body: British investors in the United States frequently invest in technology services, professional services, hospitality (particularly hotels and restaurants), creative industries, financial services, and franchises. The UK-US business relationship is among the world's most active, and many British entrepreneurs use E-2 as the vehicle for expanding an existing UK business into the U.S. market.

sections.heading: UK-US Business Corridor
sections.body: The UK is the largest single source of foreign direct investment into the United States, and British entrepreneurs frequently establish U.S. operations as a natural extension of their existing UK businesses. E-2 is particularly well-suited for British founders who want to physically relocate to manage a U.S. entity. Watson Immigration Law advises on both the visa strategy and the business structuring needed to support a successful E-2 case.

faqs.question: Did Brexit affect British nationals' ability to apply for the E-2 visa?
faqs.answer: No. Brexit had no effect on E-2 eligibility for British nationals. The E-2 visa is based on nationality (holding a UK passport), not EU membership or residence. British nationals continue to have full E-2 access under the longstanding U.S.-UK treaty relationship.

faqs.question: Do British nationals living in the EU qualify for E-2?
faqs.answer: British nationals living anywhere in the world qualify for E-2 based on their UK passport. Residency location is irrelevant — the visa is based on nationality. You would typically apply at the nearest U.S. consulate, which could be London or a U.S. consulate in your country of residence.

faqs.question: How long does the E-2 visa last for British nationals?
faqs.answer: British nationals receive a 5-year visa stamp, with each entry admission for 2 years. The visa is renewable indefinitely as long as the qualifying investment remains active.

faqs.question: Can I bring my existing UK business to the U.S. on E-2?
faqs.answer: Yes. Many British E-2 investors establish a U.S. subsidiary or affiliate of their existing UK company. The investment into the U.S. entity must meet the substantiality test, and you must own at least 50% and intend to actively manage the U.S. business.

faqs.question: What if I hold dual UK and Irish nationality?
faqs.answer: Both the UK and Ireland have E-2 treaties with the United States. You may apply using either passport. The practical differences (processing location, visa validity) may differ slightly — an immigration attorney can advise on which nationality produces the more favorable outcome for your specific case.
